title: Quantum jump trajectories, hybrid systems, non-Hermitian evolutions, quantum/classical walks
abstract: Quantum stochastic master equations of jump type are formulated in a general way and connections with quantum/classical hybrid systems and quantum filtering theory are discussed. By introducing the notion of ``typical trajectory", we show how to recursively construct the solution of the non-linear stochastic master equation (the conditional state). Moreover, by the notion of ``exclusive probability densities" we can describe all the probabilities related to the jumps, in particular, the waiting times of the jumps and their probability distributions. This general formulation and the idea of hybrid system allow to unify and generalize different fields: evolutions under non-Hermitian Hamiltonians, unitary dynamics interspersed by quantum channels at random times, quantum renewal processes, continuous time open quantum walks, Lindblad rate equation, ...
summary: This paper presents a generalized theory for quantum jump trajectories in hybrid quantum-classical systems. It demonstrates how various phenomena, such as non-Hermitian evolution and quantum renewal processes, can be unified under a single stochastic master equation framework. The work provides explicit methods to calculate the statistical properties of jumps, such as waiting times and full counting statistics.
main_problem: Developing a unified mathematical framework for quantum stochastic master equations (SME) of the jump type that incorporates hybrid quantum/classical dynamics and non-Markovian effects.
main_result: The paper establishes a general formulation that unifies non-Hermitian evolutions, quantum/classical random walks, and renewal processes, providing explicit recursive solutions for conditional states and waiting time distributions.
method: The author uses stochastic calculus (Girsanov transformations, compensated counting measures), quantum filtering theory, and the construction of 'typical trajectories' and 'exclusive probability densities' to solve non-linear SMEs.
model_or_system: A hybrid quantum/classical system where a quantum component (e.g., a qubit) is coupled to classical counting processes or random walks on graphs, governed by Lindblad-type generators and non-Hermitian effective Hamiltonians.
key_observables: Conditional (a posteriori) state, mean (a priori) state, jump statistics (waiting times, survival times, hitting times), and full counting statistics.
important_parameters: Jump intensity (lambda), stochastic intensity (I), non-Hermitian Hamiltonian parameters, and the regime of exceptional points.
main_assumptions: The dynamics of the full hybrid system are assumed to be Markovian, even if the quantum component alone exhibits non-Markovian behavior; certain point processes are simplified to time-homogeneous Poisson processes.
paper_structure: The paper begins by formulating general jump-type SMEs, moves to the construction of physical probability measures and the recursive solution of non-linear equations, then applies this framework to specific models like non-Hermitian evolutions and quantum/classical walks, concluding with mathematical proofs in the appendix.
why_interesting: It provides a powerful, unified toolkit for researchers in open quantum systems and quantum optics to describe complex trajectories, non-Hermitian dynamics, and the interplay between quantum and classical stochasticity.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks
